mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the industrial production index signal expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher expected corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following positive monthly changes in INDPRO are not significantly higher than those following negative changes over a 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 0.25 percent marks the exit from the zero lower bound and initiates an active monetary tightening impulse that raises discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in retail sales and payroll employment captures a self-reinforcing demand loop where rising income fuels spending, predicting stronger S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days than either series alone.
failure_condition: A regression of 60-day returns on RSAFS and PAYEMS shows the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) and fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns when the unemployment rate is above 6 percent because each incremental job gain signals healing economic slack and reduced recession tail-risk that is not priced when unemployment is already low.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth does not produce significantly higher 20-day forward returns when UNRATE exceeds 6 percent compared to when UNRATE is at or below 6 percent (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 500 thousand units signal a durable recovery in residential investment and construction employment, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through upstream multiplier effects on materials and consumer durables.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following months with HOUST above 500 are not significantly higher than baseline returns over a 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in both the federal funds rate and consumer prices creates a stagflationary impulse that compresses real earnings and equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between month-over-month changes in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL does not significantly predict negative 5-day returns in a multivariate regression (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative rise in the unemployment rate of at least 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals labor market deterioration that precedes weaker consumer spending and corporate revenue declines, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following the trigger are not significantly more negative than during stable or improving labor markets over a 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP indicates aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate top-line revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive surprises in GDPC1 do not exhibit a positive rank correlation with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window).
